statement: Under VS Code's higher autonomy modes (Autopilot), failed terminal commands trigger automatic retry loops without user input. This means the cost of a bad terminal pattern (heredocs, complex shell constructs) is multiplicative, not linear — a single failure can consume 5-10 retries worth of context budget before the agent tries an alternative approach. Instructions that prohibit fragile terminal patterns prevent this amplification.

evidenceRefs.title: VS Code Agent Mode — autonomy levels and auto-approval

publicSummary: Agent auto-retry under Autopilot mode makes fragile terminal patterns multiplicatively expensive. Prevention via instructions is far cheaper than cleanup after a retry spiral.
cautionNotes: Auto-retry behavior may change in future VS Code versions. Verify current autonomy mode behavior against release notes.
nonGeneralizationNotes: Specific to VS Code Copilot agent mode. Other agent runtimes may have different retry policies.
